rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in component implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings and assignments.
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are provided.
generic_rules: Use annex agree blocks to define assumptions, guarantees, and constants for AADL component contracts.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE declarations to match the actual types of expressions and features.
Define range checks using boolean expressions that relate input values to named limits or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for structural and behavioral assumptions; implementation annexes are for bindings and assignments.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level assignments in component type annexes unless they are universally valid.
Do not define range checks without visible feature names or concrete thresholds.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst component type and implementation extraction
